english: for gold. — 3. to hunt, to chase, = _čór-ba ('chor ba), ri-dwags (ri dwags) game Lex.; nya-ɣśór-ba (nya gshor ba) to fish Dzl. — 4. Sch. to cut through (?). II. adj., also ɣśér-ba (gsher ba), rough, bristly, shaggy, skra (skra), spu (spu) Stg., opp. to _jam-po ('jam po) (Sch. rough, gruff, rude?)

plough Glr., toṅ (thong), tón-ɣśol (thong gshol) id. The plough in India and Tibet consists only of a crooked beam, ɣśol-mda (gshol mda') (without wheels) with the share (ɣśol-lcágs (gshol lcags), toṅ-lcágs (thong lcags)) at the lower end; ɣśol-mdá _dzin-pa (gshol mda' 'dzin pa) to plough, lit. to take hold of the plough-beam.

1. in bgo-bśa (bgo bsha') portion, share, allowance, ration Lex., evidently a secondary form of śas (shas). — 2. Dzl. ཤྭ་, 1 inundation, flood; śwa (shwa), the reading of the manuscript of Kyelang, seems to be preferable.

pf. bśas (bshas), to slaughter, to kill (animals for food); in a story of Glr. it follows the slaughtering and must be understood to denote the cutting to pieces of the killed animal; but our Lama preferred to read bśús-pa (bshus pa) to skin.

Sch. also ɣśaṅ-ba (gshang ba), alvine discharges, bśaṅ-ba _byín-pa (bshang ba 'byin pa) to make open bowels, of food, medicines Med.; bśaṅ-dgág (bshang dgag) constipation Med.; bśaṅ-ɣcí (bshang gci) excrements and urine, bśaṅ-ɣcís skú-ba (bshang gcis sku ba) to dirty therewith Dzl.; bśaṅ-ɣcí bsri-ba (bshang gci bsri ba) to retain stool and urine Sch.; bśaṅ-lám (bshang lam) the anus Med.
leaky, leaking, full of crevices, *ču-zóm nyi-ma-la bór-na śaṅ dug* (chu zom nyi ma la bor na shang dug) W. the water-pail will become leaky, if it is left standing in the sun.

(prob. pf. of _čád-pa ('chad pa) q.v.) 1. to explain, expound; to declare, pronounce, čos-bśád-pa (chos bshad pa) to explain religion, to lecture on religious subjects, to preach; _či-ba daṅ _drar bśad ('chi ba dang 'drar bshad) he must be set down for dead Wdn.; bú-mo skyé-bar bśad (bu mo skye bar bshad) this indicates that a girl will be born Lt.; bśád-kyis mi láṅ-ṅo (bshad kyis mi lang ngo) it is ineffable, unspeakable; to say, _báb-par bśad ('bab par bshad) they say it flows down Wdn., tá-mar bśad (tha mar bshad) he is said to be on the lowest stage Thgy., ṅán-par bśad (ngan par bshad) it is said or declared to be bad, smán-du bśad (sman du bshad) it is mentioned as a medicine Wdn.; to tell, to relate, col. the usual word. — 2. to comb, v. śód-pa (shod pa). — bśad-grwa (bshad grwa) school-room, lecture-room. — *śad-dón* (shad don) W. the subject of a talk. bśad-yám (bshad yam) a public lecture Sch. (?). bśad-ló byéd-pa (bshad lo byed pa) to make many words Mil.

1. to wash, to wash out or off, to clean by washing, to rinse, plates, dishes, etc. — 2. ltó-ba bśál-ba (lto ba bshal ba) to purge the body, hence in gen. bśal-ba (bshal ba) to suffer from diarrhoea, and W. col. *śal* (shal) diarrhoea, looseness, flux, *śal rag* (shal rag) I have d., *śal dug* (shal dug) he suffers from d., *śal yoṅ* (shal yong) d. begins; bśal-_jám (bshal 'jam) a mild d., bśal-_pyés(?) (bshal 'pyes) a violent flux Sch.; bśal-nád (bshal nad) indisposition from d.; bśal-byéd (bshal byed) B., bśal-smán (bshal sman) B. and col. laxative, aperient medicine.
1. v. śud-pa (shud pa). — 2. Sch.: to purify by fire, bśúd-me (bshud me) purifying fire (?).
to put into the scabbard, to sheathe Sch.
